title: Core Spell Rules
description: Fundamental mechanics for spellcasting in D&D 5e 2024

gaining_spells.requirements: Must have the spell prepared in your mind or have access to the spell from a magic item
sources: Class features that specify spell access
Magic items such as Spell Scrolls
Special abilities that grant spell access

spell_levels.description: Every spell has a level from 0 to 9, indicating its power

0.name: Cantrips
0.description: Simple spells that can be cast almost by rote

5.name: Level 5 Spells
5.description: Very powerful magic requiring level 5+ slots

9.name: Level 9 Spells
9.description: The most powerful magic requiring level 9 slots

spell_slots.description: The main way a spellcaster's magical potential is represented

mechanics.expending_slots: When you cast a spell, you expend a slot of that spell's level or higher
mechanics.slot_sizing: A level 1 spell fits into a slot of any size, but higher-level spells need appropriately leveled slots
mechanics.restoration: Finishing a Long Rest restores any expended spell slots
mechanics.one_slot_per_turn: On a turn, you can expend only one spell slot to cast a spell

cantrips.description: A cantrip is cast without a spell slot

rituals.description: Spells with the Ritual tag can be cast as rituals

special_abilities.description: Some features allow casting specific spells without slots
special_abilities.limitation: Usually limited by usage per day or similar restrictions

magic_items.description: Spell Scrolls and other magic items contain spells
magic_items.limitation: Limited by the item's specific casting rules and charges

higher_level_casting.description: When casting a spell using a higher-level slot than required

mechanics.level_scaling: The spell takes on the higher level for that casting
mechanics.enhanced_effects: Some spells have more powerful effects when cast at higher levels

casting_in_armor.requirement: You must have training with any armor you are wearing to cast spells while wearing it
casting_in_armor.penalty: Without training, you are too hampered by the armor for spellcasting

saving_throws.calculation: Spell save DC = 8 + your spellcasting ability modifier + your Proficiency Bonus
saving_throws.mechanics: Many spells specify that a target makes a saving throw to avoid effects

attack_rolls.calculation: Spell attack modifier = your spellcasting ability modifier + your Proficiency Bonus
attack_rolls.usage: Some spells require the caster to make an attack roll to determine if the spell hits

combining_effects.different_spells: Effects of different spells add together while their durations overlap
combining_effects.same_spell: Effects of the same spell cast multiple times don't combine - only the most potent effect applies
combining_effects.equal_potency: If castings are equally potent, the most recent effect applies

identifying_spells.requirement: Spell must have ongoing duration with observable effects
